title: Complete Facial Ingest UI, Argus, debug, and manual parity
operator_request: After native Facial backend parity exists, the Atelier UI, Argus inspection, and internal manual must expose it clearly so operators and models can actually use it without foreground popups.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Use Handshake native UI/Argus/manual surfaces only. The UI must be headless-inspectable, model-addressable, and non-intrusive; it must not pop windows or steal focus.

scope.summary: Expose the completed native Facial backbone in Atelier Ingest with clear operator controls, Argus-visible state, debug/status surfaces, and internal manual instructions.
base_scope: Add/finish Atelier Ingest controls for selecting Facial feature families and profiles.
Show analysis/run status, degraded/unavailable model reasons, artifact refs, review queue status, montage/export status, and identity provenance.
Make all controls and result regions Argus-addressable with stable IDs for parallel agents.
Update internal UserManual with startup, navigation, inputs, outputs, safety constraints, failure modes, and recovery steps for Facial-backed Ingest.
Ensure the UI does not launch external windows or steal keyboard/mouse focus during model-driven testing.
Remove proxy wording where real outputs exist, while preserving explicit unavailable/degraded labels where models are absent.
acceptance_criteria: Operator can select feature families, run analysis, inspect artifacts/errors/status, and see whether identity is real or unavailable.
Argus can observe and steer all Facial Ingest controls without foregrounding the app.
Manual documents Facial purpose, core workflows, startup/run path, inputs/outputs, navigation, safety constraints, failure modes, and recovery.
No stale proxy labels remain for real implemented outputs; degraded/unavailable states remain explicit.
Controls fit at constrained desktop panel sizes without overlapping text or hidden critical state.
risks_and_controls: Risk: backend parity exists but remains invisible to operators/models. Control: every backend action needs a visible or headless control with stable Argus ID.
Risk: UI hides degraded model state. Control: tests must assert unavailable/real identity state is visible.
Risk: visual validation steals focus. Control: use Argus/headless paths only and document non-intrusive testing.
verification: Frontend interaction tests for feature selection/status/artifact/error states.
Manual content tests for Facial and Argus workflow instructions.
Argus/headless visual inspection evidence at constrained panel sizes.

manual_requirement: Required if this MT exposes or changes operator/model-visible Facial behavior.
